gaap_vs_fair_value.policy: Minerals at historical cost; production royalties off balance sheet until production

inputs.normalization_note: SSI mineral-floor + free-option. At $55, ~4.3× book — option yield falls vs $27 entry.

distribution_history.source: Special distribution estimate disclosed in 2021 special meeting materials (timberland asset sale)
Second distribution estimate disclosed in 2021 special meeting materials (timberland asset sale)

optionality_gate.notes: Economic floor ~$20.73/sh (not GAAP book); spot copper $6.3375/unit; option yield ~18.26%

base.notes: Lease income only; option largely priced in

sotp_build.notes: Lawrence base with partial production option

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Mineral rights at historical cost; fair value in Copperwood royalties [Assumption]

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage / dormant royalty?
option_scan.evidence: Copperwood lease + >1M acres; SSI mineral-floor framing

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: No operating segments; lease income only

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Royalty option not in Lawrence base cash flow

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy: Minerals at historical cost; Copperwood royalties off balance sheet until production

nav_overlay.notes: Do not use GAAP book as dhando floor

paths.label: Lawrence yield-curve / stance gate
paths.weight_why: Lawrence yield-curve or optionality_gate primary return; stance bar for accumulate.
weight_falsifiers: Gate return not updated when scenarios.base payoff/years change
Dated NAV path double-counts dividends already in yield gate

paths.label: Economic payoff dated (7yr)
paths.weight_why: Answers a different question (NAV convergence) than 7yr cash-flow IRR; capped weight to avoid theory conflation.
weight_falsifiers: NAV overlay base not updated when segment build or filings change >10%
floor_pass false but weight above 15% without [HUMAN REVIEW]

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

paths.label: NAV overlay dated payoff (7yr to overlay base)
paths.weight_why: Answers a different question (NAV convergence) than 7yr cash-flow IRR; capped weight to avoid theory conflation.
weight_falsifiers: NAV overlay base not updated when segment build or filings change >10%
floor_pass false but weight above 15% without [HUMAN REVIEW]

paths.label: Third-party approved (HK + Substacks + inventory)
paths.notes: Proxy: upside case when strategic third party has no spot IRR
paths.weight_why: Context-tier HK/Substacks until human promotes in third_party_sources.md; numeric proxy is weak.
weight_falsifiers: Using bull scenario as HK proxy when cross_check synthesis says no IRR upgrade
paths.hard_to_vary: no: proxy return is instrumentalist; prefer qualitative pp until approved blend

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

flags: NAV overlay is a different theory (asset payoff vs cash-flow IRR); weight capped at 10% to limit theory conflation.

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

cf_positive_from_2026.claim: Revenues exceed costs from 2026; self-sustaining operations
cf_positive_from_2026.filing_reconcile: H1 2025 net loss ($51,156); not in Lawrence base until confirmed

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Use one complete non-overlapping component schedule. The legacy Lawrence return path remains a separate stance gate until the primary evidence bridges are complete.

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Phase 3 scaffold identifies every currently known material operating, financial, liability, reserve, and option claim once. Primary ownership and cash/NAV bridges remain open follow-up gates.

components.label: Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows

valuation.evidence: FY2025 annual: 1,126,284 shares; >1.3M mineral acres; 36,705 leased acres. FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn. H1 2025 lease income $189K vs $149K prior year. Proof: 27.5/sh base via owner_cash_or_dividend_discount@1.0.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

source.locator: H1 2025 mineral lease income $189,101 vs $149,310 prior year; annualized ~$365K
1,126,284 shares outstanding as of 12/31/2025

assumptions.label: Duration-adjusted lease-income capitalization multiple
assumptions.rationale: Bear stresses Copperwood delay and flat lease income; base capitalizes normalized lease run-rate with modest growth optionality; bull assumes secondary lessee uplift.

calculations.label: Annual lease income per share
Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows per share

valuation.evidence: FY2025 annual: 1,126,284 shares; >1.3M mineral acres; 36,705 leased acres. FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn. H1 2025 lease income $189K vs $149K prior year. Proof: 9.9/sh base via probability_weighted_catalyst_nav@1.0.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

source.locator: 1,126,284 shares outstanding as of 12/31/2025
SSI May 2024 mineral-floor framing; ~$7.7M/yr at $4/lb copper [Assumption pending production]

assumptions.label: Copper price scale vs SSI $4/lb reference
assumptions.rationale: Base uses spot copper ~$6.54/lb vs SSI $4/lb reference; bear uses trough copper; bull uses sustained higher copper.

assumptions.label: Probability Copperwood reaches production royalty
assumptions.rationale: Low assumes project delay or failure; base matches Lawrence overlay P=35%; high assumes grant/acceleration path.

assumptions.label: Secondary acreage and water-development option value
assumptions.rationale: 667K-acre expansion and non-Copperwood leases; low zeroes undeveloped option; high assumes secondary lessee.

calculations.label: Scaled Copperwood royalty at copper price
Probability-weighted production royalty

inputs.label: Annual corporate operating burn excluding one-offs

source.locator: FY2024 MD&A; ~$160K/yr operating burn ex one-time items
1,126,284 shares outstanding as of 12/31/2025

Gross realization reserve before sign
Title, realization, and development-capital reserve per share

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of KEWL, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

component_groups.label: Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows
component_groups.economic_claim: Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows
component_groups.valuation_basis: Phase 3 direct per-share scaffold. It is a provisional valuation range, not a committee-approved price target.
component_groups.adjustments: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
component_groups.overlap_control: Unique overlap key current_operating_claim; no other component capitalizes the same claim.

component_groups.label: Copper, land, and water-development options
component_groups.valuation_basis: Phase 3 direct per-share scaffold. It is a provisional valuation range, not a committee-approved price target.
component_groups.adjustments: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
component_groups.overlap_control: Unique overlap key asset_option_inventory; no other component capitalizes the same claim.

risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Starter probability is explicit analyst judgment pending a primary-source milestone and ownership reconciliation.
risk_and_timing.timing_basis: The range includes delay and failure outcomes; dated cash realization remains [HUMAN REVIEW].
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital, financing, and dilution are reserved until the primary sources-and-uses bridge is complete.

component_groups.label: Cash and other net claims

component_groups.overlap_control: Unique overlap key net_financial_claims; no other component capitalizes the same claim.

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

valuation_method_route.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

low.label: Copper price scale vs SSI $4/lb reference
low.rationale: Base uses spot copper ~$6.54/lb vs SSI $4/lb reference; bear uses trough copper; bull uses sustained higher copper.

low.label: Probability Copperwood reaches production royalty
low.rationale: Low assumes project delay or failure; base matches Lawrence overlay P=35%; high assumes grant/acceleration path.

low.rationale: 667K-acre expansion and non-Copperwood leases; low zeroes undeveloped option; high assumes secondary lessee.

economic_ownership_map.evidence: FY2025 annual: 1,126,284 shares; >1.3M mineral acres; 36,705 leased acres. FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn. H1 2025 lease income $189K vs $149K prior year. Proof: 9.9/sh base via probability_weighted_catalyst_nav@1.0.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: The range includes delay and failure outcomes; dated cash realization remains [HUMAN REVIEW].
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Starter probability is explicit analyst judgment pending a primary-source milestone and ownership reconciliation.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital, financing, and dilution are reserved until the primary sources-and-uses bridge is complete.

method_route.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash
method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

required_refresh_triggers: new filing or material operating update

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material claim is valued exactly once by a valid calculation proof and every material evidence blocker is resolved.
